message:
feat(cli): assign watch overlay auto-binding, bind refactoring, mouse support

Three related CLI improvements:

1. Assign watch overlay integration (assign.go, assign_pure_test.go):
   - runWatchMode() now calls prepareAssignWatchOverlay() to auto-install
     F12 overlay binding when running inside the target tmux session
   - Pure function design with injected dependencies for testability
   - 197 new test lines covering shouldOfferAssignWatchOverlay,
     buildAssignWatchOverlayHint, buildAssignWatchOverlayWarning,
     prepareAssignWatchOverlay with all code paths (outside tmux,
     existing binding, missing binding, setup failure)

2. Bind command refactoring (bind.go):
   - Extract setupOverlayBindingQuiet() (no stdout) for use by assign
     watch overlay integration
   - Extract setupOverlayBindingWithWriter(), overlayBindingCommand(),
     overlayBindingArgs(), maybeFprintf(), isOverlayBindingLine()
   - The quiet variant suppresses stdout so the watch mode output
     is not polluted with binding setup messages

3. Mouse support for palette and tutorial (palette.go, tutorial.go):
   - Both commands now enable tea.WithMouseCellMotion() unless NTM_MOUSE=0
   - Consistent mouse support across all interactive TUI surfaces

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
